summary: Developer Preview npm publish is complete for the free package set. The root compatibility package remains stable on latest and all publishable v2 packages are available under next.

completedEntries.result: Published react-native-chart-kit@7.0.0-next.0 to npm under the next dist-tag while preserving react-native-chart-kit@latest at 6.12.2.
Published @chart-kit/core@7.0.0-next.0, @chart-kit/svg-renderer@7.0.0-next.0, and @chart-kit/react-native@7.0.0-next.0 under the next dist-tag.
Verified Developer Preview publish state with npm run release:publish:status -- --strict.
Confirmed @chart-kit/pro@7.0.0-next.0 and @chart-kit/skia-renderer@7.0.0-next.0 remain unpublished for Developer Preview.

notes: npm keeps latest=7.0.0-next.0 on the new scoped @chart-kit packages because they have no stable version yet. The strict publish-state checker allows this only when no stable version exists and still fails if an existing stable package latest tag is moved to a prerelease.
Existing react-native-chart-kit users are protected from accidental upgrades because react-native-chart-kit@latest remains 6.12.2 and the v2 Developer Preview is installed with the next dist-tag.
